title: Patagonia 14-Day Nature Retreat
metaTitle: Patagonia 14-Day Nature Retreat | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Patagonia, Argentina: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Perito Moreno Glacier boardwalk park entry and Perito Moreno mini-trekking. Prices from $2814 per person (estimate).
highlights: Perito Moreno Glacier boardwalk park entry
Los Glaciares National Park (El Chaltén sector)
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Patagonia, Argentina — Cold, windy, sub-polar climate; summer highs around 18-20°C (Dec-Feb) with long daylight hours, and winter highs near 2-5°C (Jun-Aug) with strong winds, limited daylight, and reduced tourist services. December to February, the Southern Hemisphere summer, for the best weather for glacier trekking and hiking; the region is far south and effectively winds down in winter. This itinerary blends Perito Moreno Glacier boardwalk park entry, Perito Moreno mini-trekking, Los Glaciares National Park (El Chaltén sector) with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Perito Moreno Glacier boardwalk park entry, Perito Moreno mini-trekking, Los Glaciares National Park (El Chaltén sector)
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Patagonia
itinerary.description: Land at Comandante Armando Tola International Airport (El Calafate) (FTE) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Perito Moreno Glacier boardwalk park entry — Los Glaciares National Park viewing platforms. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Perito Moreno Glacier boardwalk park entry

itinerary.title: Patagonia: Perito Moreno mini-trekking & Nautical safari boat to the glacier face
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Perito Moreno mini-trekking — Guided ice-trekking with crampons on the glacier. In the afternoon, continue to Nautical safari boat to the glacier face — Close-up boat approach to the ice wall. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Nautical safari boat to the glacier face

itinerary.title: Patagonia: Fitz Roy guided trekking tour (El Chaltén) & Glaciarium Ice Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Fitz Roy guided trekking tour (El Chaltén) — Guided hike toward the Fitz Roy massif. In the afternoon, continue to Glaciarium Ice Museum — Interactive museum on Patagonian glaciers, with an ice bar. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Fitz Roy guided trekking tour (El Chaltén)

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Perito Moreno Glacier
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Perito Moreno Glacier, a popular side trip from Patagonia. Return to Patagonia in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Perito Moreno Glacier

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Laguna Torre trek in El Chaltén — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Laguna Torre trek in El Chaltén

itinerary.title: Patagonia: Upsala Glacier boat excursion & Laguna Nimez Nature Reserve
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Upsala Glacier boat excursion — Boat trip past drifting icebergs on Lago Argentino. In the afternoon, continue to Laguna Nimez Nature Reserve — Wetland reserve with flamingos near El Calafate. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to El Chaltén and Fitz Roy
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to El Chaltén and Fitz Roy, a popular side trip from Patagonia. Return to Patagonia in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: El Chaltén and Fitz Roy

itinerary.title: Patagonia: El Calafate Historical Interpretation Center & Torres del Paine day trip (Chile)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to El Calafate Historical Interpretation Center — Regional history and Patagonian settlement museum. In the afternoon, continue to Torres del Paine day trip (Chile) — Full-day cross-border excursion to the famous towers. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Estancia Cristina remote ranch — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Torres del Paine National Park (Chile)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Torres del Paine National Park (Chile), a popular side trip from Patagonia. Return to Patagonia in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Torres del Paine National Park (Chile)

itinerary.title: Patagonia: Estancia gaucho day tour & Los Glaciares National Park (El Chaltén sector)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Estancia gaucho day tour — Sheep ranch visit with a gaucho asado lunch. In the afternoon, continue to Los Glaciares National Park (El Chaltén sector) — Free-entry trailhead town for Fitz Roy hikes. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Los Glaciares National Park (El Chaltén sector)

itinerary.title: Patagonia: Fitz Roy base trail views & Avenida del Libertador
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Fitz Roy base trail views — Self-guided hiking trails with no park fee. In the afternoon, continue to Avenida del Libertador — Main street of El Calafate town. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Walichu Caves ancient rock art — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to El Calafate lakefront promenade or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to FTE for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Perito Moreno mini-trekking and Fitz Roy guides well ahead in peak summer (Dec-Feb)
Pack for extreme wind — Patagonia is famous for it
Most El Chaltén trails are free and self-guided, with no permit needed for day hikes
Expect reduced services and hours in the off-season (May-Sep)
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Heavy-duty windproof and waterproof outer layers, even in summer
Sunglasses and high-SPF sunscreen for the thin ozone layer at this latitude
Thermal base layers for sudden temperature drops
thingsToDo: Perito Moreno Glacier boardwalk park entry
Nautical safari boat to the glacier face
Fitz Roy guided trekking tour (El Chaltén)
Los Glaciares National Park (El Chaltén sector)
Multi-day Fitz Roy trekking in El Chaltén
thingsToAvoid: Underestimating Patagonian wind on exposed trails and viewpoints
Hiking Fitz Roy or Torres del Paine circuits without checking weather and daylight windows
Traveling in deep winter when many tours and roads run reduced service
Skipping layers — temperatures swing fast between sun and shade
topRestaurants: Isabel Cocina al Disco — Argentine home-style ($$)
Pura Vida El Calafate — Home-style Argentine ($$)
Olivia Café & Deli — Cafe and light bites ($)
Viva la Pepa (El Chaltén) — Argentine casual ($$)
instagramSpots: Fitz Roy reflected in Laguna de los Tres
hiddenGems: Laguna Torre trek in El Chaltén
adventureActivities: Multi-day Fitz Roy trekking in El Chaltén
luxuryExperiences: Stay at a luxury estancia lodge with all-inclusive excursions
Private guided helicopter flight over Fitz Roy
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset boat cruise to Perito Moreno Glacier
Private estancia dinner under the stars
Secluded lodge stay overlooking Lago Argentino
budgetExperiences: Free self-guided hikes around El Chaltén
Wander El Calafate's free lakefront promenade
Budget hostel-based day hikes toward Fitz Roy

festivalCalendar.name: Día de la Tradición (Gaucho Heritage Day)
Semana de Santa Cruz provincial celebrations
Marcha Blanca winter torchlight festival (broader Patagonia)

localEtiquette: A single cheek kiss is a common greeting, even among new acquaintances.
Tipping around 10% is appreciated in restaurants.
Follow Leave No Trace principles in national parks.
Dress in warm layers regardless of the season — weather shifts fast.
If offered mate (a shared herbal tea), accept it as a social gesture.
localTransportGuide: El Calafate itself is small and walkable, but reaching Perito Moreno Glacier (80km) or El Chaltén (215km) requires a rental car, an organized bus tour, or the regular Chaltén Travel shuttle bus. Distances in Patagonia are vast, so build significant travel time into any itinerary.
bestTimeToVisit: December to February, the Southern Hemisphere summer, for the best weather for glacier trekking and hiking; the region is far south and effectively winds down in winter.
weather: Cold, windy, sub-polar climate; summer highs around 18-20°C (Dec-Feb) with long daylight hours, and winter highs near 2-5°C (Jun-Aug) with strong winds, limited daylight, and reduced tourist services.
visaInformation: Argentina allows visa-free tourist entry for up to 90 days for most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia); no advance visa is needed for most nationalities for short tourist stays.
averageFlightTime: ~18.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
nearestAirport: Comandante Armando Tola International Airport (El Calafate) (FTE)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Patagonia?
faqs.answer: Argentina allows visa-free tourist entry for up to 90 days for most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia); no advance visa is needed for most nationalities for short tourist stays.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Patagonia?
faqs.answer: December to February, the Southern Hemisphere summer, for the best weather for glacier trekking and hiking; the region is far south and effectively winds down in winter. Cold, windy, sub-polar climate; summer highs around 18-20°C (Dec-Feb) with long daylight hours, and winter highs near 2-5°C (Jun-Aug) with strong winds, limited daylight, and reduced tourist services.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Patagonia safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Patagonia is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Patagonia?
faqs.answer: The local currency is ARS. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Priya Rossi
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Laguna Nimez Nature Reserve in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Daniel Novak
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Fitz Roy base trail views and Torres del Paine day trip (Chile). Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Ethan Nguyen
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Avenida del Libertador and Estancia gaucho day tour. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: David Patel
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Patagonia is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Torres del Paine day trip (Chile). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.body: Upsala Glacier boat excursion alone was worth the trip, but combined with Torres del Paine day trip (Chile) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
